note: IMPORTANT: Always use v1.md (crisp-only format) for this widget

variationStrategy.description: Generate 13 data variations covering normal, edge, missing-field, zero-nonaccepted, and no-prior-schedule cases.

categories.description: Balanced sign-off distribution across 4 schedules and 4 risk levels.
One schedule has very high pending or rejected proportions.
Some rows contain 0 pending or 0 rejected to test bolding rules.

signoffStatusBySchedule.rows has exactly 1 item — the current schedule only (scheduleTag='Current'). NO previous schedules exist at all. meta.schedulesIncluded has 1 entry. Tests how the narrator handles zero trend context — no oldest/previous schedule to compare against. Should describe current metrics only without referencing any trend.
Very high closureHealthScore across all schedules.
Very low closureHealthScore and high nonAcceptedPct across schedules.
Some optional fields are null or missing in rows.
Every row across all 3 sections has 0 pending and 0 rejected — 100% accepted, closureHealthScore=100. Tests zero-bolding rules: no pending/rejected should be bolded, no nonAcceptedPct to bold.

signoffStatusBySchedule.rows has exactly 2 items: one previous (scheduleTag='Previous 1') and one current. No Previous 2 or Previous 3 rows. Tests how the narrator handles minimal trend context with only 1 comparison point.

2 rows: the single previous schedule is 100% accepted (closureHealthScore=100.0, nonAcceptedPct=0), current schedule has significant pending and rejected. Tests comparison narrative when baseline is perfect but current is degraded.
